vulnerability.reference: Low health literacy combined with automation complacency makes users accept false negatives as truth.
vulnerability.generated: Users' inability to critically evaluate multimodal evidence fosters over-trust in explainable automated flags, enabling acceptance of false negatives.

impact.reference: Harmful health misinformation spreads unchallenged, leading to vaccine hesitancy and risky health behaviors.
impact.generated: Unchecked propagation of harmful health misinformation causes preventable illness, treatment avoidance, and strain on public health systems.

dose_response.reference: Explainable yet erroneous fact-checking outputs create false credibility, which with low health literacy and automation complacency leads social media users to accept false negatives, allowing harmful health misinformation to spread.
dose_response.generated: Explainable yet erroneous multimodal fact-checking outputs, through social media users relying on automated health claim flags, trigger inability to critically evaluate evidence, fostering over-trust and acceptance of false negatives, leading to unchecked harmful health misinformation causing preventable illness and public health strain.
